seoTitle: Ashbourne vs Ashwood — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Ashbourne and Ashwood. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Ashbourne and Ashwood.
heading: Ashbourne vs Ashwood — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Ashbourne, Victoria and Ashwood, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Ashbourne has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Ashwood (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Ashbourne or Ashwood safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Ashbourne has a flood risk score of 53/100, while Ashwood scores 50/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Ashbourne scores 20/100 for bushfire risk. Ashwood scores 49/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Ashbourne or Ashwood?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Ashbourne has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.7% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
